articles.title: Worse fall than 1987, $69 billion wipeout as IBM ‘falters’: Why stock price crashed 25% - analysts call it ‘ugly moment’
articles.summary: ​​Shares of IBM plunged 25% on Tuesday, with the company seeing a steeper one-day decline than it experienced during the 1987 "Black Monday" market crash. The weakness also spread to other software stocks.

articles.title: Stock market today: BSE Sensex jumps 450 points, NSE Nifty50 trades above 24,100
Gold Silver Rate Today Live Updates: Gold rallies over 2% to $4,063 as weak US data boosts investor sentiments
IBM shares tank 26% after initial results disappoint
articles.summary: International Business Machines reported disappointing preliminary results on Tuesday. This news caused significant drops in software and IT services company shares. Investors are now concerned about a capital spending pivot impacting the entire industry.

articles.title: Air transport down 14% in April: ISP data
articles.summary: Air transport output contracted nearly fourteen percent in April, reflecting high fuel costs. Railways also saw a slight decline in output during the same month. Fourteen of nineteen services sub-sectors registered double-digit growth, showing overall resilience.

articles.title: Oil price today: Crude tops $85 a barrel as US restores Strait of Hormuz blockade
articles.summary: Oil prices surged beyond eighty-five dollars per barrel on Wednesday. This occurred after the United States reinstated a naval blockade on Iran. Fighting between Washington and Tehran escalated, raising concerns over crude supplies.

articles.title: Singapore court denies stay on Byju's jail term
articles.summary: Singapore's high court denied Byju Raveendran's request to suspend his contempt sentence. The court lifted temporary protection for the Byju's founder while his appeal is pending. His legal team clarified the hearing focused only on the stay application.

articles.title: NCLT okays record no. of cases, flags manpower shortage
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal approved a record 78 resolution plans in April-June. This period saw approvals valued at Rs 5,518 crore, reaching nearly Rs 4.8 lakh crore total. The tribunal faces significant institutional capacity constraints and member vacancies.

articles.title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for July 15, 2026 - check list
articles.summary: Stock market recommendations: Route Mobile, Tata Power, and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories - these are the top stocks to buy picked by Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares for July 15, 2026.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Steve Jobs: ‘Older people sit down and ask...’
articles.summary: Steve Jobs (1955-2011) was an American entrepreneur, inventor, and business magnate best known as the co-founder of Apple. Jobs played a key role in revolutionising personal computing, smartphones, digital music, and animation.

articles.title: Wholesale inflation touches 27-month high of 9.9% in June
articles.summary: Wholesale inflation reached a 27-month high of 9.9 percent in June. This surge was driven by rising food prices and energy costs. Food prices accelerated to 5.5 percent while mineral prices rose significantly.

articles.title: With 73% rise, Adani Properties top realty gainer
articles.summary: Adani Properties saw significant wealth surge, moving to fourth position on the property list. DLF retained its top valuation despite a notable decline in its overall worth. Lodha Developers and Indian Hotels Company also experienced valuation decreases this year.

articles.title: Raising bank stakes gets easier for DIIs
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed changes to bank shareholding norms. These amendments aim to ease investment by domestic institutional investors. Governance rules will also be revised for bank boards to focus on strategy.

articles.title: Led by NRI deposits, RBI swaps may draw $80-85 billion
articles.summary: Bankers anticipate India will attract eighty to eighty-five billion dollars through RBI's swap-backed initiatives. Early inflows are expected from FCNR(B) deposits, which are already seeing traction.

articles.title: IDBI stake sale back on table, government keen to speed up process
articles.summary: Government is accelerating the IDBI Bank stake sale process. Two bidders, Fairfax and Emirates NDB, are being considered. This follows the rejection of earlier bids below the reserve price. Recent foreign investments in Indian banking include Yes Bank and RBL Bank.

articles.title: 'Hello, this is Divya': How one WhatsApp message allegedly cost a 70-year-old Gwalior CA ₹21 crore
articles.summary: On December 25, 2025, Vijayvargiya allegedly transferred ₹10,000 four times through UPI. A few days later, around ₹1 lakh was transferred through a friend's UPI account.

articles.title: FTA with UK comes into effect today, exports to get a boost
articles.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement begins implementation, boosting Indian exports to the UK. Indian service providers and professionals will gain greater access to the United Kingdom market.

articles.title: UK-India FTA goes live today: From whisky to premium cars, what gets cheaper and which Indian sectors gain
articles.summary: The FTA gives 99% of Indian exports duty-free access to the UK, while India has selectively reduced tariffs on British goods over a phased period. New Delhi has, however, protected sensitive domestic sectors

articles.title: India moves to block imports linked to forced labour amid USTR probe
articles.summary: India is inserting a new clause into its foreign trade policy. This clause will allow blocking imports produced using forced labour. The government will use the International Labour Organisation's definition of forced labour.

articles.title: US eases Russia sanctions bill, cuts tariff threat on India and China from 500% to 100%
articles.summary: US senators unveiled a revised Russia sanctions bill targeting major energy buyers. This legislation seeks to pressure Moscow and its key oil and gas importers. The updated bill reduces previously proposed tariffs on top purchasers like China and India.

articles.title: India Post crosses Rs 4,000 crore quarterly revenue for first time; Q1 turnover jumps 22%
articles.summary: India Post achieved its highest-ever first-quarter revenue of Rs 4,008 crore. This revenue marks a significant 22.2 percent increase from the previous year. The parcel and mail segments showed substantial revenue growth during this period.

articles.title: RBI officials meet top bankers. Here's key issues that were discussed
articles.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra urged banks to leverage advanced technologies, including AI, to improve operational efficiency and enhance customer experience, while also ensuring robust cybersecurity,

articles.title: Trump admin’s Section 301 probe: Why India’s ban on import of goods produced through use of forced labour is important
articles.summary: The move comes against the backdrop of a US investigation into forced labour practices across 60 economies, including India. The notification said the new provisions will take effect after the completion of 30 days from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.

articles.title: Export boost, cheaper cars & whisky: India-UK trade deal comes into effect from July 15 - How India & Indians will benefit
articles.summary: With its implementation, the trade deal will become the sixth free trade agreement brought into force during the Narendra Modi government's tenure.

articles.title: RBI proposes easier bank share acquisition rules for MFs, insurers, pension funds: What changes
articles.summary: RBI has proposed easing share acquisition norms for mutual funds, insurance companies and pension funds by introducing a one-time approval mechanism for repeat stake purchases in banks.

articles.title: Govt releases new monthly Services Production Index; Here's what the first data shows
articles.summary: Seen as a proxy of GVA, covers 14 of 19 sub-sectors for now

articles.title: Assam's debt surged 4.5-fold to ₹1.62 lakh crore in 10 years, government tells Assembly
articles.summary: As per the latest information from the Accountant General, the total debt of Assam stood at 1,61,761 crore as on March 31, 2025

articles.title: Amid US probe, India set to prohibit import of goods manufactured using forced labour
articles.summary: DGFT notifies new para in Foreign Trade Policy, creating legal framework, effective in 30 days

articles.title: 'Best ones were never the loudest': Bear Grylls calls PM Modi among world's 'most powerful leaders'
articles.summary: Bear Grylls praised PM Narendra Modi in a social media post on leadership, sharing a throwback picture from their Man vs Wild shoot along with images of Barack Obama and Prince William.

articles.title: Who is Siddhartha Saxena? IIT Kanpur alumnus who claims he earned ₹76.99 crore in one day
articles.summary: The tech entrepreneur, who co-founded AI startup Merlin with his IIT Kanpur batchmates, reflected on his journey from a small Indian town to building multi-million-dollar companies in the US.

articles.title: India-UK FTA to boost trade, investments; bilateral commerce may double to $120 billion by 2030: ASSOCHAM
articles.summary: The industry body said the landmark trade pact will provide duty-free access to 99% of India’s exports to the UK while opening 89.5% of India’s tariff lines to British goods.

articles.title: Love Gulab Jamun? This Brazilian treat is the viral dessert you need to know about
articles.summary: The dessert shot to fame after Instagram creator Sunidhi (@nids.lid) shared a simple recipe video that has amassed more than 27 million views.

articles.title: India, Maldives conclude first round Of FTA talks, aim to deepen trade and investment ties
articles.summary: India remains the second-largest trading partner of the Maldives. Bilateral trade reached $771.76 million in the 2025-26 financial year, registering a 13.54% increase from the previous year

articles.title: How Sai Pallavi’s Coimbatore and Kotagiri Residences Reflect Her Life Journey
articles.summary: Sai Pallavi’s Coimbatore and Kotagiri homes reflect her grounded lifestyle, traditional elegance, and cinematic journey from medical graduate to pan-Indian star with INR 45–50 Cr net worth.

articles.title: Travelling by train? This ₹80,000 verdict against Railways could change what you're entitled to
articles.summary: The commission observed that the immediate reporting of the theft to the RPF, contradictory positions taken by the Railways, the absence of the coach attendant

articles.title: Singapore court declines Byju Raveendran's application to stay jail sentence
articles.summary: This term essentially barred Raveendran from returning to Singapore unless he is prepared to serve the term.

articles.title: Affordable Luxury: How ₹2 Lakh Made This 7-Seater the Ultimate Family MPV
articles.summary: Discover how a ₹2 lakh upgrade transforms a standard Maruti Ertiga into a luxury 7-seater family MPV with premium interiors, tech features, and head-turning design.

articles.title: Nuclear Giants: Who Really Holds the World’s Deadliest Arsenals
articles.summary: Explore the world’s deadliest nuclear arsenals, from Russia’s 5,580 warheads to India’s 180, revealing global power dynamics, strategic deterrence, and geopolitical stakes.

articles.title: What Really Happens If You’re Lost in Space
articles.summary: Being lost in space exposes the human body to vacuum, hypoxia, radiation, micrometeoroids, and extreme cold, making survival impossible and creating eerie, preserved remains.

articles.title: The Hidden Home Appliances Sabotaging Your Wi-Fi Signal
articles.summary: Hidden appliances like microwaves, fish tanks, mirrors, and electronics can weaken your Wi-Fi signal, causing slowdowns or dead zones. Simple fixes restore connectivity and speed.
